steps.title: Map the disagreement
steps.content: The students see the novel's letter format as daunting and its gender treatment as old-fashioned. The professor counters that the language sounds modern and the issues still matter today.

steps.title: Choose the professor's point
steps.content: Choice A compares the novel with other books from the same period, which the passage does not do. Choice C compares it with contemporary friendship novels, which is unsupported. Choice D overstates the note about possible reading orders. The remaining choice captures the professor's point that the novel is more current than it first appears. The answer is B.
